summary: Frontend-only smoke + e2e testing of StreamVault landing page. All critical user flows verified: hero, product cards (Spotify/Netflix/YouTube), plan selector pricing changes, Buy Now -> cart drawer, qty +/-, remove, subtotal/savings, cart badge, checkout dialog with mocked ~1.6s simulation, success state, success-close clears cart, empty cart state, smooth-scroll nav links, testimonials (3 reviews with avatars), FAQ accordion, footer. Zero console/page errors during entire flow.

issues: Individual feature cards lack data-testid attributes (e.g., why-feature-*). Section renders correctly but per-feature testability is missing. Minor maintainability concern.

action_items: (Optional/minor) Add data-testid attributes to WhyChooseUs feature cards (e.g., data-testid="why-feature-0" ... "why-feature-5") to align with the testability convention used elsewhere.

(Optional) Consider closing the cart drawer cleanly before opening the CheckoutDialog. Currently the Sheet data-state remains 'open' briefly after setIsOpen(false) due to Radix exit animation. Visually fine; only surfaces in automated tests.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is clean and well-structured; CartContext correctly memoizes subtotal/savings/count with useMemo and useCallback.
CheckoutDialog: form validation (isValid) is reasonable but card length >= 12 may allow invalid card numbers. Acceptable since checkout is explicitly MOCKED client-side.
Subscriptions.jsx: plan selector & discount math correct. Hover/glass styles are well executed.

CartDrawer: All required data-testid attributes present (cart-drawer, cart-item-<key>, increase/decrease/remove/qty-<key>, cart-subtotal, cart-savings, checkout-button, empty-cart, continue-shopping-button).
No backend API calls; checkout is FULLY MOCKED via setTimeout(1600ms). This matches the user's stated requirement (no auth, mock checkout).
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a single-page React landing site at /. Backend is original boilerplate (not used). Checkout is MOCKED (setTimeout 1.6s). All required data-testid hooks exist and match the spec. When automating cart drawer button clicks via Playwright, prefer JS-evaluated clicks (element.click()) over Playwright .click() because Radix Sheet's transform causes some buttons to be reported as 'outside viewport' even when visible. Hero headline visually reads 'Unlock Premium Streaming for Less.' but text_content concatenates lines without a space — use case-insensitive substring contains for assertion.
verified_flows: All 3 product cards render with correct data-testids
Plan selector 1m/3m/12m updates price correctly ($4.99/$12.99/$39.99 for Spotify)
Buy Now adds item AND opens cart drawer
Cart shows correct subtotal ($9.98 with qty=2) and savings ($14.00)
Checkout button opens CheckoutDialog (cart drawer closes)
Form accepts email/name/card/exp/cvc; success shown after ~1.6s
